metadata.author: Gad and Nathan
metadata.subject: The Furtherance of the Transition from the Period of the Judges to the Period of the Kings.

1: And after the death of Saul, when David had returned from the slaughter of the Amalekites, David stayed two days in Ziklag.
And on the third day, a man came from the camp, from Saul, with his clothes torn and earth upon his head. And when he came to David, he fell to the ground and did obeisance.
And David said to him, Where do you come from? And he said to him, I have escaped from the camp of Israel.
2: And after this David inquired of Jehovah, saying, Shall I go up into one of the cities of Judah? And Jehovah said to him, Go up. And David said, Where shall I go up? And He said, To Hebron.
So David went up there, and his two wives also, Ahinoam the Jezreelitess and Abigail the wife of Nabal the Carmelite.

2.text: The act of falling to the ground in respect. This scene sets the stage for David's reaction to the news of Saul's death, showing his respect for the 'anointed of Jehovah' despite Saul's persecution of him.

1.text: Hebron was a major city in Judah and a place of significant spiritual history (the burial place of Abraham). It became David's first capital where he reigned over Judah for seven years.
